zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert Allen於1306年出生在英國英格蘭時，他的父親Thomas Allen26歲，母親Mabella Maunsell24歲。他至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert Allen于1306年出生在英国英格兰时，他的父亲Thomas Allen26岁，母亲Mabella Maunsell24岁。他至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: Allen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Allenby
meanings: English: habitational name from Allonby (Cumberland). The placename is a post-Conquest compound of the French (Breton) personal name Alein (see Allen ) + Old Norse bȳ ‘village’, or the same word borrowed into Middle English.

variants.name: Allin
meanings: English (Devon): variant of   Allen  .
Swedish: variant of   Ahlin  .

variants.name: Alano
meanings: Italian (mainly Campania) and Hispanic (mainly Philippines): from the personal name Alano (see Allen ).

variants.name: Alan
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Allen  . This is the usual form of the personal name in England and Scotland.

variants.name: Allan
meanings: Scottish and northern English: variant of   Allen  . This is the more common form of the name in Scotland and northern England. See also   McAllen  .

History: An early bearer of this surname in North America was William Allan, a British soldier who took his family to the newly founded city of Halifax, NS, Canada, in 1749. His son John (1746–1805) was a Revolutionary soldier who eventually (1783) founded the settlement of Allan's Island, Passamaquoddy Bay, ME.

variants.name: Allain
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean-Paul, Adelard, Alban, Albenie, Alphonse, Andree, Cyrille, Donat, Emile, Euclide, Francois.

Breton and French: from the Old Breton personal name Alan (Old French Alain); see Allen . In France the personal name is spelled Alain, and this is also a less common variant of the surname (see Alain ).
English: variant of   Allen  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Alen
meanings: English: variant of   Allen  .
Galician (Alén): habitational name from one of the many places called Alén in Galicia (Spain), from Galician alén ‘beyond’.
Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from   Ale  .

variants.name: Allis
meanings: English: from the Middle English female personal name Alis, Alice (Old French Adeliz, Ahelis, Aalis, Aliz). Alice, together with its pet form Alison, was one of the most popular names in both England and Scotland from the 13th century until the end of the medieval period. The personal name is of ancient Germanic origin, brought to England from France by the Normans; it is a shortened form of ancient Germanic Adalheidis, which is composed of the elements adal ‘noble’ + haid ‘‘rank, worth’’.

variants.name: Alleyne
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Chantal, Julien, Marcell.

English: variant of   Allen  . This is an old-fashioned spelling, already established as a surname in England in Tudor times. Today this surname is found mainly in the West Indies, especially Barbados.

variants.name: Alling
meanings: English: either a variant of Allen , or a variant of Halling with loss of initial H-.
German: habitational name from either of two places called Alling, one in Bavaria and one in Austria.
Danish: habitational name from any of several places called Alling. The placename may be a derivative of al ‘alder’.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Allen was born in 1306, in England, United Kingdom, his father, Thomas Allen, was 26 and his mother, Mabella Maunsell, was 24. He had at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Allen est né en 1306, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas Allen, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Mabella Maunsell, avait 24 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert Allen 1306 in England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Allen 26 und seine Mutter Mabella Maunsell 24. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Allen è nato il nel 1306 nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Thomas Allen, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Mabella Maunsell, aveva 24 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert Allenが1306年にイギリスイギリスで生まれた時，父Thomas Allenは26歳，母Mabella Maunsellは24歳であった。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert Allen(이)가 1306년 영국 잉글랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Allen(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Mabella Maunsell(은)는 24세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Allen nasceu em 1306, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Thomas Allen, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Mabella Maunsell, tinha 24 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Allen родился в 1306 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Thomas Allen было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Mabella Maunsell, 24. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert Allen nació en 1306, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Thomas Allen, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Mabella Maunsell, tenía 24 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo.
